sourceMovie.title: Timeline
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Timeline (2003) as a action theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The action and adventure blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: John Carter
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Timeline clicked for you, John Carter (2012) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and adventure, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Timeline clicked for you, The Time Machine (2002) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and adventure, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Timeline?
faq.answer: Try John Carter, The Time Machine, Stargate: Continuum—each shares DNA with Timeline while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Timeline?
faq.answer: Timeline (2003) is best described as Action, Adventure, Science Fiction. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Timeline worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 5.7/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this action lane.
